---
title: "Frank-Wolfe on the polytope"
subtitle: "Linear minimisation oracle, no projections"
---

## Envelope gradient

The outer problem $\min_{\boldsymbol{t} \in \mathcal{T}_k} f_\delta(\boldsymbol{t})$ is a smooth optimisation, but $f_\delta$ is itself the value of an *inner* minimisation over $\boldsymbol{\beta}$. Differentiating it directly would mean tracking how $\hat{\boldsymbol{\beta}}(\boldsymbol{t})$ moves as $\boldsymbol{t}$ changes — a chain through the inner solver.

The **envelope theorem** (Danskin) sidesteps this. Because $\hat{\boldsymbol{\beta}}(\boldsymbol{t})$ is optimal for the inner problem, those derivative terms cancel, and the gradient of $f_\delta$ has a closed form in terms of $\hat{\boldsymbol{\beta}}(\boldsymbol{t})$ alone:

$$
\boxed{\;\;
\bigl[\nabla f_{\delta}(\boldsymbol{t})\bigr]_j
\;=\; -\,\frac{2\,\delta\,\hat\beta_j(\boldsymbol{t})^{2}}{t_j}.
\;\;}
$$

One ridge-GLM solve gives $\hat{\boldsymbol{\beta}}(\boldsymbol{t})$; the gradient is then $O(p)$ arithmetic — no implicit differentiation through the inner solver.

## The Frank-Wolfe iteration

At iterate $\boldsymbol t^{(r)}$:

1. **Linear minimisation oracle (LMO)**:

$$
\boldsymbol s^{(r)} \;\in\;
\arg\min_{\boldsymbol u \in \mathcal{T}_k}
\langle \boldsymbol g(\boldsymbol t^{(r)}),\,\boldsymbol u\rangle
\;\;\Longleftrightarrow\;\;
\boldsymbol s^{(r)} = \text{vertex with the $k$ smallest entries of $\boldsymbol g(\boldsymbol t^{(r)})$.}
$$

2. **Step**: $\boldsymbol t^{(r+1)} = (1 - \alpha)\,\boldsymbol t^{(r)} + \alpha\,\boldsymbol s^{(r)}.$

3. **Iterate** until $\boldsymbol t^{(r)}$ converges to a vertex.

## Why no projections

The cardinality polytope is a *combinatorial* set; projecting onto it is itself a hard problem. Frank-Wolfe avoids the projection — every step just requires the LMO, which is a sort.

## Vertex-approach guarantee

$$
\|\boldsymbol t^{(r)} - \boldsymbol s^*\|_\infty
\;\le\;
(1 - \alpha)^r \,\|\boldsymbol t^{(0)} - \boldsymbol s^*\|_\infty.
$$

Linear convergence to the chosen vertex once the LMO direction settles.

> Placeholder for visual: a small 3D figure of FW iterates marching toward a vertex of the polytope.

::: {.page-nav}
[← Previous: Boolean relaxation](01-relaxation.qmd)

[Next: Homotopy →](03-homotopy.qmd)
:::
